tweets.title: Pakistan's sudden surge in rhetoric over the Indus Waters Treaty is likely driven by multiple factors not just one. My assessment: ✅ Genuine concern over water security & India's growing leverage. ✅ International narrative-building & diplomatic pressure. ✅ Domestic political messaging. ✅ Preparing the groundwork to justify future hostile actions. The bigger story, however, is India's decision to keep the treaty in abeyance until Pakistan credibly and irrevocably ends support for cross-border terrorism. That decision has introduced long-term strategic uncertainty. Even before India builds major new hydraulic infrastructure, that uncertainty itself acts as a source of leverage.
tweets.content:
Pakistan's sudden surge in rhetoric over the Indus Waters Treaty is likely driven by multiple factors not just one.
My assessment:
✅ Genuine concern over water security & India's growing leverage.
✅ International narrative-building & diplomatic pressure.
✅ Domestic political messaging.
✅ Preparing the groundwork to justify future hostile actions.
The bigger story, however, is India's decision to keep the treaty in abeyance until Pakistan credibly and irrevocably ends support for cross-border terrorism.
That decision has introduced long-term strategic uncertainty. Even before India builds major new hydraulic infrastructure, that uncertainty itself acts as a source of leverage.

tweets.title: BREAKING: HM Amit Shah Urges Early Report from Demographic Change Panel Union Home Minister Amit Shah has asked the High-Level Committee on Demographic Changes to submit its recommendations at the earliest. The committee, chaired by Justice (Retd.) Prakash Prabhakar Naolekar, has begun consultations with states, Union Territories, and Central ministries to examine demographic changes linked to illegal immigration and other abnormal factors. Its mandate includes: 🔹 Studying demographic shifts across India, especially in border districts, metro cities, industrial towns, and tribal regions. 🔹 Assessing the impact on national security, governance, and social cohesion. 🔹 Recommending policy, legal, and administrative measures, including mechanisms for the identification, detention, and deportation of illegal immigrants. The committee was constituted by the Ministry of Home Affairs following Prime Minister Narendra Modi's Independence Day 2025 announcement and is expected to submit its final report within one year.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: HM Amit Shah Urges Early Report from Demographic Change Panel

Union Home Minister Amit Shah has asked the High-Level Committee on Demographic Changes to submit its recommendations at the earliest.

The committee, chaired by Justice (Retd.) Prakash Prabhakar Naolekar, has begun consultations with states, Union Territories, and Central ministries to examine demographic changes linked to illegal immigration and other abnormal factors.

Its mandate includes:
🔹 Studying demographic shifts across India, especially in border districts, metro cities, industrial towns, and tribal regions.
🔹 Assessing the impact on national security, governance, and social cohesion.
🔹 Recommending policy, legal, and administrative measures, including mechanisms for the identification, detention, and deportation of illegal immigrants.

The committee was constituted by the Ministry of Home Affairs following Prime Minister Narendra Modi's Independence Day 2025 announcement and is expected to submit its final report within one year.

VIJAY stands for: V – Vigilance & Readiness I – Innovation & Transformation J – Jointness & IntegratioA – Aatmanirbharta (Self-Reliance) Y – Yoddha First (Soldier First) Gen Seth said the Army will focus on operational readiness, technological innovation, tri-service integration, indigenous capability development, and the welfare of every soldier, from the newest Agniveer to the most senior veteran, while supporting the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047.
tweets.content:
ARMY CHIEF UNVEILS 'VIJAY' VISION FOR A FUTURE-READY INDIAN ARMY

Chief of the Army Staff Gen Dhiraj Seth outlined his strategic roadmap for the Indian Army under the acronym "VIJAY", aimed at building a technology-enabled, future-ready and self-reliant force.

VIJAY stands for:
V – Vigilance & Readiness
I – Innovation & Transformation
J – Jointness & IntegratioA – Aatmanirbharta (Self-Reliance)
Y – Yoddha First (Soldier First)

Gen Seth said the Army will focus on operational readiness, technological innovation, tri-service integration, indigenous capability development, and the welfare of every soldier, from the newest Agniveer to the most senior veteran, while supporting the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047.

tweets.content:
BREAKING: Pakistan Opposition Leaders Blocked from Entering Pakistan Occupied Kashmir (PoK)

The Jammu Kashmir Joint Awami Action Committee (JKJAAC) has accused Pakistani authorities of suppressing democratic rights after a delegation of opposition leaders was reportedly stopped from entering Pakistan-occupied Jammu & Kashmir (PoJK).

According to JKJAAC, the delegation, led by Mahmood Khan Achakzai and including former Prime Minister Shahid Khaqan Abbasi and other opposition figures, was halted at Kotli and prevented from proceeding further.

The committee also alleged restrictions on movement and supplies, describing them as part of a broader crackdown on dissent. JKJAAC reiterated that its movement is peaceful and vowed to continue protests until its demands are addressed.

tweets.title: General Upendra Dwivedi concludes his tenure as the 30th Chief of Army Staff, handing over command to General Dhiraj Seth. His tenure marked a distinct operational shift for the Indian Army, transitioning it toward a highly modernized, outcome-driven force. Key highlights from his leadership: - The Five Pillars: Gen Dwivedi executed a transformation framework focused on technology absorption, agile structural reorganization, and advanced human resource upskilling. - Tech & Self-Reliance: Championed indigenous defense manufacturing, overseeing the integration of roughly 50,000 drones alongside AI, big data, and quantum tech into daily operations. - Combat Readiness: Validated new doctrines and maintained strict vigilance through major deployments, notably Operation Snow Leopard on the northern borders and Operation Sindoor on the western front. - Tri-Service Synergy: Accelerated joint interoperability among the Army, Navy, and Air Force, laying critical groundwork for theaterization. - Specialized Units: Raised specialized combat formations explicitly trained to handle multi-domain threats and mass drone warfare.
tweets.content:
General Upendra Dwivedi concludes his tenure as the 30th Chief of Army Staff, handing over command to General Dhiraj Seth. His tenure marked a distinct operational shift for the Indian Army, transitioning it toward a highly modernized, outcome-driven force.

Key highlights from his leadership:

- The Five Pillars: Gen Dwivedi executed a transformation framework focused on technology absorption, agile structural reorganization, and advanced human resource upskilling.

- Tech & Self-Reliance: Championed indigenous defense manufacturing, overseeing the integration of roughly 50,000 drones alongside AI, big data, and quantum tech into daily operations.

- Combat Readiness: Validated new doctrines and maintained strict vigilance through major deployments, notably Operation Snow Leopard on the northern borders and Operation Sindoor on the western front.

New : 🚨“Declaration at Rawalakot: ‘PoJK Is Not Part of Pakistan.”

Thousands of demonstrators at Rawalakot’s Eidgah Ground reiterated that PoJK is not part of Pakistan, delivering another major challenge to Islamabad’s control over the region.

Since 9 June, another group of protesters has been staging a sit-in near the Line of Control (LoC). Addressing the gathering, Aman Khan hinted that locals could seek closer ties with India if the blockade continues.

“If the Pakistani establishment continues to block food, let it be known that the remaining borders of PoJK could open up. If that happens, Islamabad will find itself begging the people of PoJK to stay,” Khan said.

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

BREAKING: Arunachal Tribal Body Alleges Chinese Encroachment

The Nah Welfare Society (NWS) in Arunachal Pradesh has alleged that the PLA has gradually occupied parts of its traditional grazing, hunting, and agricultural land in Upper Subansiri district over the past six years.

According to a memorandum submitted to district authorities, the group claims Chinese forces have established roads and military camps in several areas under the Taksing revenue circle.

No official confirmation has been issued by the district administration, the state government, or the Government of India. Local MLA Nakap Nalo has said the allegations require official verification, calling it a matter of national security.
